text.title: Planning-Layer AI Spec Workflow
text.summary: Agent workflow for spec.md + implementation-plan.md generation at the SDD planning layer, including prompt patterns, subagent recommendations, and gotchas.

rule.statement: Separate the spec generation call from the implementation plan generation call — never combine both in one prompt.
rule.rationale: Mixing both tasks in one prompt degrades quality for both outputs. The planning phase requires the spec to be complete and human-approved before task breakdown begins. Combining creates a situation where the plan is based on an unreviewed spec.

rule.statement: Never proceed from spec generation to implementation plan without an explicit human approval signal on the spec.
rule.rationale: Planning agents cannot self-assess whether a spec is good enough. The human approval gate is the quality signal that enables `faion-feature-executor` to start executing tasks. Without it, agents execute against incorrect specifications.

list.item: Agent reads feature intent or backlog item
Agent asks structured clarifying questions before generating spec
Agent generates draft spec.md: FR list, edge cases, Given-When-Then ACs, dependencies, out-of-scope
Agent generates implementation-plan.md: phase breakdown, task list, token estimates, dependency order
faion-feature-executor picks up approved implementation plan
faion-sdd-execution: validate spec quality against SDD patterns before human review
faion-feature-executor: downstream executor of approved implementation plans
faion-brainstorm: diverge-converge ideation for ambiguous features with uncertain requirements

rule.statement: After an agent refines a spec based on feedback, always diff the before/after FR list — agents silently drop FRs that seem "implied" by the feedback.
rule.rationale: This is one of the most destructive planning-layer failure modes. An agent editing spec "to address feedback about performance" may remove FRs it judges as performance-adjacent — without flagging the removals. The human must verify the FR count is stable or removals are intentional.

list.item: Implementation plan tasks frequently lack dependency order — validate graph before execution
Task granularity is often too coarse: "implement authentication" is not a single agent task
Token estimates are approximations — actual consumption varies by codebase complexity
Agents will not proactively flag missing domain context — explicitly prompt: "list all assumptions made"

text.reference: XML prompt: draft spec.md + implementation-plan.md from feature intent
XML prompt: refine spec based on reviewer feedback with FR preservation
Shell script: scaffold spec.md from Linear issue via GraphQL API
